title: JIT (correctness): Different `UnboundLocalError` for same code with JIT on or off
author: devdanzin
body:
# Bug report

### Bug description:

The following code results in two different `UnboundLocalError` exceptions depending on whether it's run with the JIT on or off:

```python
def f():

    if 0:
        class Class:
            pass

    if 0:
        instance = Class()

    for _ in range(5000):
        try:
            _ = instance.f()
        except Exception:
            pass
    Class.__bases__ = (object,)

f()
```

With JIT on, the `UnboundLocalError` points to `'instance'`, while with JIT off it points to `'Class'`.

Traceback with JIT on:
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/danzin/divergences/divergence_child_6010_3_1.py", line 17, in <module>
    f()
    ~^^
  File "/home/danzin/divergences/divergence_child_6010_3_1.py", line 12, in f
    _ = instance.f()
        ^^^^^^^^
UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'instance' where it is not associated with a value
```

Traceback with JIT off:
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/danzin/divergences/divergence_child_6010_3_1.py", line 17, in <module>
    f()
    ~^^
  File "/home/danzin/divergences/divergence_child_6010_3_1.py", line 15, in f
    Class.__bases__ = (object,)
    ^^^^^
UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'Class' where it is not associated with a value
```

Output for `PYTHON_LLTRACE=4`:
[divergence_01_lltrace.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22903957/divergence_01_lltrace.txt)

Output for `PYTHON_OPT_DEBUG=4`:
[divergence_01_opt_debug.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22903958/divergence_01_opt_debug.txt)

Please let me know whether you're able to reproduce this issue.

Found using [lafleur](https://github.com/devdanzin/lafleur/).

### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-140233
* gh-140687
* gh-140890
* gh-141495
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: It's possible https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139109 might end up fixing this inadvertently, so let's not fix this for now maybe? Or we could fix it with the knowledge the code will be ripped out soon.

comments.author: devdanzin
efimov-mikhail
comments.body:
We need a backport for 3.14, don't we?
Or we ignore backports since JIT is in expiremental stage?

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: Backport failed, maybe I should open up the backport to anyone who wants to try please?
